title: Tutto � Sciolto
content:
A birdless heaven, seadusk, one lone star
    Piercing the west,
    As thou, fond heart, love's time, so faint, so far,
    Rememberest.

    The clear young eyes' soft look, the candid brow,
    The fragrant hair,
    Falling as through the silence falleth now
    Dusk of the air.

    Why then, remembering those shy
    Sweet lures, repine
    When the dear love she yielded with a sigh
    Was all but thine?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, James Joyce delivers a powerful performance in "Tutto � Sciolto"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
